memory_candidates.content: Synthesis fork pattern for team research: The lead should NOT read all teammate output files inline (anti-pattern accumulating 4-12k tokens). Instead, fork an anonymous agent with the teammate file paths as @-references. The synthesis agent reads files in its own fresh context, performs conflict detection and gap analysis, writes the unified report, and returns a ~200-word summary. Lead context grows by ~250 tokens instead of 7-21k tokens.

When team skills (skill-team-research, skill-team-plan) need to synthesize outputs from multiple parallel teammates, dispatch a named synthesis-agent rather than having the lead read all files inline. The synthesis-agent runs in a fresh context, reads all teammate finding files as @-references, and returns a compact summary (~200 words) to the lead. This keeps lead context growth at ~900 tokens instead of 7-21k tokens. The synthesis-agent is at .claude/agents/synthesis-agent.md and has allowed-tools: Read, Write only.

active_projects.completion_summary: Refactored skill-team-research to use named synthesis-agent for context protection. Lead context growth for synthesis drops from 7-21k to ~900 tokens. Created synthesis-agent.md (218 lines), extracted teammate prompts to team-wave-helpers.md, replaced Stages 7-9 with handoff-metadata collection and synthesis-agent dispatch, migrated postflight to skill-base.sh, updated system registration in CLAUDE.md and merge-source.

Applied context-protective lead pattern to 7 skills: removed format spec injection (cat -> @-reference), delegated memory retrieval to subagents, tightened prose in skill-orchestrator, delegated synthesis in team skills.

Added optional prompt parameter to /orchestrate command: updated both command files with Stage 0 arg parsing, updated skill to extract and thread focus_prompt through all 5 dispatch sites, updated CLAUDE.md command reference.

active_projects.description: Add optional prompt parameter to /orchestrate command. Current syntax: /orchestrate N. New syntax: /orchestrate N [prompt]. The prompt flows through the delegation chain: command parses args into task_number + prompt, includes prompt in delegation context JSON, skill extracts prompt and propagates to each sub-agent dispatch (research, plan, implement). Files to modify: .claude/commands/orchestrate.md, .claude/extensions/core/commands/orchestrate.md, .claude/skills/skill-orchestrate/SKILL.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md command reference.
active_projects.completion_summary: Added optional free-text prompt parameter to /orchestrate command that flows through the delegation chain to all sub-agent dispatches (research, plan, implement, resume, and blocker re-implement). Both command files (active + extension copy), the skill state machine, and CLAUDE.md command reference were updated.

active_projects.description: 18 scripts in .claude/scripts/ are missing from .claude/extensions/core/scripts/ and the core manifest provides.scripts array. This causes the <leader>al loader to not copy them to other projects, breaking all commands (which reference command-gate-in.sh, parse-command-args.sh, skill-base.sh, etc). Fix: copy the 18 missing scripts to the extension source directory and add them to manifest.json. Critical missing scripts: command-gate-in.sh, command-gate-out.sh, command-route-skill.sh, parse-command-args.sh, skill-base.sh, dispatch-agent.sh, generate-task-order.sh, archive-task.sh, vault-operation.sh, roadmap-sync.sh, roadmap-integration.sh, memory-harvest.sh, orphan-detection.sh, issue-grouping.sh, postflight-workflow.sh, rename-session.sh, tier-selection.sh, validate-context-budgets.sh.
active_projects.completion_summary: Copied 18 missing scripts from .claude/scripts/ to .claude/extensions/core/scripts/ with permission preservation and updated manifest.json provides.scripts array from 26 to 44 entries in alphabetical order. Full parity confirmed: zero diff, valid JSON, 44 manifest entries matching 44 filesystem files.

artifacts.summary: All three implementation agents share advisory Stage 4D-ii post-phase review. Phase marked COMPLETED before review runs, allowing unchecked items to pass. Fix: count-and-gate validation at insertion points in general (lines 197-225), neovim (173-182), nix (199-208) agents.
3-phase plan to add count-and-gate post-phase validation to all implementation agents and backport check-off instructions to neovim and nix agents

active_projects.description: Add lightweight post-phase validation to implementation agents that checks all - [ ] items in the current phase were addressed (completed, skipped with annotation, or deferred) before marking the phase [COMPLETED]. Currently the agent is instructed to check off items (Stage 4B-ii) but nothing validates compliance. Add a self-check step between Stage 4D (post-phase review) and Stage 5 (next phase) that counts unchecked items and either completes them or annotates why they were skipped. Files: general-implementation-agent.md, neovim-implementation-agent.md, nix-implementation-agent.md.

Have the orchestrator inspect the plan file between cycles (not just the handoff) to detect drift and decide if plan revision is needed. Currently the orchestrator only reads .orchestrator-handoff.json after each agent dispatch. With structured counts from task 613, add logic in skill-orchestrate SKILL.md to: (1) read phase completion percentages from the handoff, (2) if subtask completion < 70% in a phase, inspect the plan file for unchecked items vs. annotated deviations, (3) if significant drift detected (>30% deviations), trigger plan revision via reviser-agent before continuing. Files: skill-orchestrate SKILL.md (post-dispatch logic, lines 306-327).
